perturbation.original_text: The obligations under this Agreement shall be perpetual (unless otherwise provided for herein) or until such time as the information is no longer considered Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII and a written notice to that fact is provided to the Designa ted Individual and/or the Contractor by the Executive Director of the TJPA.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations under this Agreement shall be for a period of five (5) years from the date of this agreement, regardless of whether the information is no longer considered Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII, unless terminated earlier by mutual written consent of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction by setting a fixed term (five years) for the confidentiality obligations, overriding the original perpetual term tied to the classification of the information and contradicting section 8. This conflicts with the clause allowing termination based on the information's classification change, creating ambiguity and potential disputes over the duration of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Duration And Survival Of Confidentiality Obligations. The obligations under this Agreement shall be for a period of five (5) years from the date of this agreement, regardless of whether the information is no longer considered Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII and a written notice to that fact is provided to the Designa ted Individual and/or the Contractor by the Executive Director of the TJPA. 9. Severability. Each provision of this Agreement is severable and, if a court should find any provision of this Agreement to be unenforceable, all other provisions of this Agr eement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a fixed five-year term for confidentiality obligations, overriding the original perpetual term tied to the classification of the information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which also specifies a five-year term, but the original text it was based from considered the classification of the information. The contradiction arises because the change removes the condition based on information classification, creating conflicting conditions for the confidentiality obligations and creating uncertainty over enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: Remedies: Any Contractor or Designated Individual that violates the Specificat ion with respect to Confidential Information, SSI and/or PCII shall, at the TJPA’s election, be subject to any of the following: (1) written reprimand of the Contractor and/or Designated Individual; (2) termination of the Contractor or Designated Individual’s access to Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII; (3) directive to the Contractor to immediately suspended the Designated Individual from performing any further work relating to the Project; (4) if a contract has been awarded, treatment of the unautho rized disclosure of Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII as a material breach of the contract, warranting the termination of the contract for cause; (5) commencement of legal proceedings for any other legal rights and remedies, including injunctive relie f and monetary damages arising from the breach of contract; (6) reporting of any violation related to SSI or PCII to the Department of Homeland Security, the Transportation Security Administration, or the Department of Transportation (these agencies may also independently assess penalties and/or take corrective action as provided under Federal law, including 49 C.F.R. §§ 15.17 and 1520.17); (7) disqualification of a Contractor from bidding on any work for the Project; and/or (8) written directive to retriev e all Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII disclosed to unauthorized persons at Contractor’s expense and to cease future unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Remedies: Any Contractor or Designated Individual that violates the Specificat ion with respect to Confidential Information, SSI and/or PCII shall only be subject to a written reprimand. All other remedies are waived.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by limiting the remedies for violating the Specification to only a written reprimand, contradicting the original section that listed several other potential remedies, including termination of access, suspension from work, and legal proceedings. This introduces ambiguity and legal uncertainty about the actual consequences of violating the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Violations And Remedies. Unauthorized Disclosure: If a Contractor or any Designated Individual knows or has a reasonable belief that documents subject to the Specification have been or may be disclosed to an unauthorized person(s), the Contractor and/or Designated Individual shall imme diately notify the TJPA. The TJPA shall make the final determination as to whether a violation of the Specification occurred and the course of action to address the violation. Remedies: Any Contractor or Designated Individual that violates the Specificat ion with respect to Confidential Information, SSI and/or PCII shall only be subject to a written reprimand. All other remedies are waived. If legal proceedings to enforce any remedy set forth under this paragraph are commenced by the TJPA, the prevailing party in such legal action shall be entitled to reasonable attorney’s fees and costs . Furthermore, Contractor agrees to indemnify the TJPA for any expenses or damages (including attorney’s fees or costs) incurred by the TJPA arising out of or relating to the Contractor and/or Designated Individual’s unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly limits the remedies for violating the Specification to only a written reprimand and waives all other remedies. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which both outline a range of potential remedies, including termination of access, suspension from work, legal proceedings, and financial responsibility. The change creates a direct conflict, making the remedies section inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. The Perturbation Explanation accurately reflects this direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the United States that I have read, understood, and will comply, in full, with the obligations set forth under this Non -Disclosure Agreement for Bidders and Contractors.
perturbation.changed_text: I declare that I have skimmed through, and will attempt to comply, with best effort, with the obligations set forth under this Non -Disclosure Agreement for Bidders and Contractors.
perturbation.explanation: The change contradicts the original agreement by weakening the commitment to full compliance. Originally, the Designated Individual declared they 'will comply, in full.' Now, it is downgraded to 'will attempt to comply, with best effort', this creates an in-text contradiction in the obligation undertaken by the individual in section 11, generating a conflict and potentially disputes over liability for non-compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Modification. This Agree ment may not be changed, modified, discharged, or terminated, except by an instrument in writing signed by the Executive Director of the TJPA or her designee. ACCEPTED AND AGREED: I declare that I have skimmed through, and will attempt to comply, with best effort, with the obligations set forth under this Non -Disclosure Agreement for Bidders and Contractors. DESIGNATED INDIVIDUAL NAME: (typed/printed) ________________________________ SIGNATURE _______________________________________ TITLE: ____________________________________________ CONTRACTOR: ________________________ (EMPLOYER) EMAIL: _______________________________ DATE: ________________________________ ACKNOWLEDGED: The Designated Individual above is an employee, officer, director, agent, representative, subcontractor, or consultant that has been designated by the Contractor as a Designated Individual of the Contractor and is duly authorized to execute this Agreement. I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the United States that the foregoing is true and correct. CONTRACTOR NAME: (typed/printed) ________________________________
perturbation.justification: The original text states a declaration of full compliance with the Non-Disclosure Agreement. The changed text modifies this declaration to state that the individual has merely 'skimmed through' the agreement and will only 'attempt to comply, with best effort'. This creates a direct contradiction within the document because Section 11 includes the statement 'ACCEPTED AND AGREED: I declare that I have skimmed through, and will attempt to comply, with best effort, with the obligations set forth under this Non -Disclosure Agreement for Bidders and Contractors', which undermines the original agreement's intention of full compliance, thus triggering an in-text contradiction in the obligation undertaken by the individual, leading to potential disputes over liability for non-compliance as mentioned in the Perturbation Explanation. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong inconsistency and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The Designated Individual further represents and warrants that he/she is an employee, officer, director, agent, representative, subcontractor, or consultant that has been designated by the Contractor as a person with a need to kno w the Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII to prepare and submit a bid for a portion of the work and/or perform work at the Project, and is fully authorized to enter into this Agreement by the Contractor.
perturbation.changed_text: The Designated Individual represents that he/she may or may not be directly employed by the Contractor, but is assisting in the preparation of a bid and therefore needs to review Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII. The Contractor acknowledges responsibility for the Designated Individual's actions related to this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction by creating uncertainty about the Designated Individual's employment status. Originally, the individual represented being an employee, officer, etc. Now, they may or may not be directly employed, conflicting with section 4. This ambiguity affects the Contractor's liability and responsibility under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Representations And Warranties. The Designated Individual represents and warrants that he/she has reviewed, understands, and is familiar with the TJPA’s Document Control Procedure, the Contractor’s Document Control Procedure, and the standards for protecting Confidential Information, SSI , and PCII as set forth in the TJPA’s Document Control Procedure and as set forth in the relevant laws, regulations, and/or directives applicable to the specific categories of information to which the Designated Individual may be granted access. The Desig nated Individual further represents and warrants that he/she understands that the TJPA may conduct inspections, at any time or place, for the purpose of ensuring compliance with the conditions for access, dissemination, handling, and safeguarding informati on under this Agreement. The Designated Individual represents that he/she may or may not be directly employed by the Contractor, but is assisting in the preparation of a bid and therefore needs to review Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII. The Contractor acknowledges responsibility for the Designated Individual's actions related to this agreement. 5. Requests For Disclosure By Third Parties. If a subpoena, discovery request, Court order, Freedom Of Information Act Request, or any other request or demand authorized by law seeking disclosure of the Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII is received by the Designated Individual, individually or on behalf of the Contractor, the Designated Individual shall immediately notify the TJPA thereof in order to permit the TJPA to investigate the circumstances, prepare any appropriate documentation, seek to quash the subpoena, to seek a protective order, or to take such other action regarding the request as it deems appropriate; and the undersigned will fully cooperate in the TJPA’s efforts in this regard.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction with section 4. The original text specifies that the Designated Individual 'is an employee, officer, director, agent, representative, subcontractor, or consultant'. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating the Designated Individual 'may or may not be directly employed by the Contractor'. This directly contradicts the representations and warranties in Section 4 regarding the Designated Individual's status and the Contractor's responsibility, creating a conflict regarding the Designated Individual's status.
